name: Ospedale San Raffaele - MAGICS Center
description_long: The MAGICS (MAGnetic IntraCerebral Stimulation) Center at IRCCS Ospedale San Raffaele is a leading neuromodulation research and clinical center within the Institute of Experimental Neurology. The center conducts pioneering TMS research for multiple sclerosis, chronic neuropathic pain, migraines, Alzheimer's disease, fatigue, depression, and stroke rehabilitation. San Raffaele is one of Italy's most prestigious research hospitals, and the MAGICS Center represents its commitment to advancing non-invasive brain stimulation therapies.
machines: Transcranial Magnetic Stimulator (clinical and research grade)

doctors_data.name: Prof. Letizia Leocani
doctors_data.title: Associate Professor of Neurology, Director of MAGICS Center
doctors_data.bio: Associate Professor of Neurology at Vita-Salute San Raffaele University and Director of the Neurorehabilitation Unit and the MAGICS Center at IRCCS San Raffaele Hospital. After obtaining her MD in Milan and PhD in Human Physiology, she specialized in Neurology and was a Visiting Research Fellow at the National Institutes of Health in Bethesda, Maryland. Her research focuses on non-invasive neurostimulation including TMS and tDCS for central nervous system diseases.

name: Fondazione Santa Lucia IRCCS
description_long: Fondazione Santa Lucia IRCCS is the leading research center in Italy and among the top five in the world for TMS research, preceded only by University College London, Harvard University, National Institutes of Health, and University of Toronto. The center's Experimental Neuropsychophysiology Laboratory employs transcranial magnetic stimulation and transcranial direct current stimulation techniques for research and clinical applications. Groundbreaking studies here have demonstrated that TMS can slow Alzheimer's disease progression by 52% and significantly improve memory, stroke recovery, and cognitive function.

doctors_data.name: Prof. Giacomo Koch
doctors_data.title: Research Director, Director of Non-Invasive Brain Stimulation Laboratory
doctors_data.bio: Neurologist and Research Director at Fondazione Santa Lucia IRCCS since 2006, and Professor of Physiology at the University of Ferrara since 2020. Among the top 10 international experts in transcranial magnetic stimulation. His research focuses on clinical neurophysiology of the motor system and cognitive functions, with over 469 publications and 21,691 citations. Led the landmark study showing 52% slowing of Alzheimer's progression with TMS treatment.

name: Healthy Brain Institute
description_long: The Healthy Brain Institute is a cutting-edge Italian center dedicated to brain health, specializing in Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation (TMS). The institute offers non-pharmacological treatment based on the application of electromagnetic fields capable of acting on brain areas whose functioning is altered by certain pathologies. TMS has been shown to be extremely effective for treating mood depression, substance abuse and alcohol dependence, and chronic pain.

doctors_data.name: Prof. Giacomo Koch
doctors_data.title: Scientific Advisor, Neurologist
doctors_data.university: University of Ferrara / Fondazione Santa Lucia IRCCS
doctors_data.bio: World-renowned expert in TMS and non-invasive brain stimulation, also affiliated with Fondazione Santa Lucia IRCCS. His leadership at the Healthy Brain Institute bridges advanced neuroscience research with clinical TMS practice in Rome.

name: Brain Stimulation Italia - Mood Center Milano
description_long: Brain Stimulation Italia operates advanced TMS treatment centers offering rTMS, tDCS, and Deep rTMS therapies. The Milan Mood Center, located in the Navigli area, specializes in treating psychiatric disorders including depression, eating disorders, substance dependencies, OCD, bipolar disorder, and chronic pain without hospitalization. Scientific studies demonstrate that the technology used leads to improvement in 85% of cases, with over 80% of patients completing the therapy cycle successfully overcoming their conditions. Treatment cycles consist of 20 sessions lasting 20 minutes each.

doctors_data.name: Dott. Giuseppe Fazzari
doctors_data.title: Scientific Director, Psychiatrist
doctors_data.bio: Psychiatrist with 40 years of experience, Scientific Director of Brain Stimulation Italia. Former Director of the Psychiatry Unit 23 at Montichiari Hospital for 25 years. Leads the clinical application of rTMS and other brain stimulation therapies for psychiatric disorders and addiction treatment.

doctors_data.name: Dott.ssa Maria Chiara De Vivo
doctors_data.title: Clinical Coordinator, Milan Center
doctors_data.bio: Coordinates the multidisciplinary team of psychiatrists, psychologists, and technicians at the Milan Mood Center, overseeing patient care and treatment protocols for brain stimulation therapies.

name: Brain Stimulation Italia - Mood Center Brescia
description_long: Brain Stimulation Italia's Brescia center is located within Poliambulatorio PTC and offers the same advanced rTMS, tDCS, and Deep rTMS treatments as the Milan center. Under the scientific direction of Dr. Giuseppe Fazzari, the center provides non-invasive brain stimulation for psychiatric disorders, addiction, and chronic pain. The Brescia location was among the first Brain Stimulation Italia centers established.
address: c/o Poliambulatorio PTC, Via Fratelli Ugoni, 2

doctors_data.name: Dott. Giuseppe Fazzari
doctors_data.title: Scientific Director, Psychiatrist
doctors_data.bio: Psychiatrist with 40 years of experience and Scientific Director of Brain Stimulation Italia. Leads clinical operations and treatment protocols at the Brescia Mood Center.

name: Brain&Care Milano
description_long: Brain&Care is a specialized clinic for the treatment of dependencies and mental wellness, offering Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation (TMS) as a non-invasive brain stimulation therapy. Led by Prof. Antonello Bonci, a world authority in addiction treatment, the clinic's team of psychiatrists, psychologists, and technicians provides cutting-edge diagnosis and treatment for psychiatric and neurological disorders. Brain&Care adopts evidence-based protocols combining TMS with comprehensive psychological support.

doctors_data.name: Prof. Antonello Bonci
doctors_data.title: Scientific Director
doctors_data.bio: World authority in the treatment of addiction disorders and one of the leading experts in the TMS field. Scientific Director of Brain&Care, leading an international team of medical professionals, psychologists, and technicians with consolidated experience in neuroscience and mental health.

name: Brain&Care Torino
description_long: Brain&Care Torino, located within the CDC Kormed healthcare facility on Piazza Statuto, offers TMS therapy for addiction and mental health disorders in Turin. The clinic provides the same evidence-based Brain&Care protocols for brain stimulation combined with comprehensive psychological care, serving the Piedmont region.

name: Brain&Care Rimini
description_long: Brain&Care Rimini provides TMS therapy and mental wellness services on Corso d'Augusto in the heart of Rimini. The clinic offers non-invasive brain stimulation treatments for addiction and psychiatric disorders, serving the Emilia-Romagna region with the Brain&Care evidence-based treatment approach.

name: Istituto Auxologico Italiano - rTMS Milano
description_long: Istituto Auxologico Italiano is a prestigious IRCCS (research hospital) in Milan offering repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) and transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS/sDCS) within its Department of Neurorehabilitative Sciences. The rTMS rehabilitation programs are applied as an advanced non-invasive brain stimulation technique for motor and cognitive disorders and rare forms of chronic pain. Treatment is available at multiple Auxologico locations in Milan including Capitanio, Procaccini, and Pier Lombardo.

doctors_data.name: Prof. Luigi Tesio
doctors_data.title: Director, Department of Neurorehabilitative Sciences
doctors_data.bio: Physiotherapist and Director of the Department of Neurorehabilitative Sciences at Istituto Auxologico Italiano, overseeing the rTMS and tDCS treatment programs for motor and cognitive rehabilitation.

name: Casa di Cura Parco dei Tigli
description_long: Casa di Cura Parco dei Tigli is a private neuropsychiatric clinic in Teolo (Padova province) offering TMS therapy among its comprehensive mental health services. The clinic provides both SSN-accredited and private hospitalization for neuropsychiatric conditions, with a serene parkland setting in the Euganean Hills area near Padova.

name: Brainclinics Foundation
description_long: Research Institute Brainclinics was founded in 2001 as an independent research institute in Nijmegen, specializing in advancing our understanding of the brain through applied neuroscience. Brainclinics was the first in Europe to treat depression with TMS in an outpatient setting. Their expertise extends to the development and application of brain stimulation techniques including rTMS for depression and OCD, TMS-induced Heart-Brain Coupling, and EEG-based precision psychiatry. The institute also organizes the annual TMS Masterclass and Certification Course, accredited by the Dutch-Flemish Brain Stimulation Foundation.

doctors_data.name: Dr. Martijn Arns
doctors_data.title: Founder and Director, Associate Professor
doctors_data.university: Utrecht University (PhD), Maastricht University (Associate Professor), Visiting Scholar at Stanford University
doctors_data.bio: Biological-psychologist and entrepreneur who founded Brainclinics in 2001. Received his PhD at Utrecht University on EEG-based personalized medicine for ADHD and depression. Specialized in neurobiological aspects of ADHD and depression, stratified psychiatry, biomarkers, and neuromodulation techniques such as TMS. Over 190 scientific publications and four patents. Currently a visiting scholar at Stanford University's Brain Stimulation Lab.

name: Neurowave Amsterdam
description_long: Neurowave is a specialized practice in Amsterdam's Jordaan neighborhood offering innovative, evidence-based rTMS therapy for patients with depression and obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD). The multidisciplinary team includes a psychiatrist, two GZ-psychologists, three psychologists, and a neurobiologist. Intake appointments are generally possible within 1 to 2 weeks. The treatment is reimbursed by Dutch health insurers.

name: Amsterdam UMC - rTMS Psychiatry
description_long: Amsterdam UMC (located at VUmc campus) offers rTMS as a scientifically proven, effective, and safe treatment for depression, reimbursed by Dutch health insurers since 2017. The Department of Psychiatry provides rTMS in combination with psychotherapy, typically 20 to 30 sessions over 5 to 12 weeks. As a leading academic medical center, Amsterdam UMC conducts cutting-edge research on brain stimulation therapies alongside clinical treatment.

name: GGZ inGeest - Neuromodulation
description_long: GGZ inGeest offers rTMS and ECT neuromodulation treatments for patients with treatment-resistant depression and bipolar depression. The center combines research with regular treatment, currently studying the effectiveness of rTMS for depressive symptoms in bipolar disorder. Approximately 30% of clients with treatment-resistant depression achieve full remission after a complete rTMS treatment course. The treatment is covered by Dutch health insurance.

doctors_data.name: Dr. Eric van Exel
doctors_data.title: Psychiatrist, Lead Investigator Neuromodulation
doctors_data.bio: Psychiatrist with expertise in neuromodulation and rTMS for treatment-resistant depression. Lead investigator for the T-Bide study on rTMS effectiveness for depressive symptoms in bipolar disorder at GGZ inGeest.

name: rTMS International Rotterdam
description_long: rTMS International is one of the Netherlands' leading dedicated rTMS treatment providers with over 10 years of experience. The Rotterdam clinic, located on 's-Gravendijkwal, offers combined rTMS and psychotherapy for depression and obsessive-compulsive disorder. The clinic has a dedicated team of psychologists and psychiatrists specialized in integrating brain stimulation with talk therapy for optimal outcomes.

name: TMS Center Europe
description_long: TMS Center Europe specializes in effective, medication-free rTMS treatment for depression and obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCS/OCD). Based in Rotterdam, the center provides evidence-based rTMS programs combined with psychotherapy. The center serves patients from across Europe and offers treatment in both Dutch and English.

name: neurocare Clinics Amsterdam
description_long: neurocare Clinics Amsterdam is part of the international neurocare group network, offering combined treatment of psychotherapy with rTMS magnetic brain stimulation for depressive disorders and OCD. The clinic uses evidence-based protocols including rTMS, neurofeedback, tDCS, and QEEG brain mapping. Since May 2024, rTMS combined with psychotherapy is officially recommended in the updated Dutch multidisciplinary guideline for Depression.

name: neurocare Clinics Den Haag
description_long: neurocare Clinics Den Haag, situated on the prestigious Lange Voorhout in The Hague, offers rTMS therapy combined with psychotherapy for depression and OCD. The clinic provides a comprehensive suite of neuromodulation services including rTMS, neurofeedback, tDCS, QEEG brain mapping, and actigraphy sleep assessments. Located 15 minutes walking distance from Den Haag Centraal station.

doctors_data.name: Els Graafsma
doctors_data.title: Clinical Psychologist / Psychotherapist
doctors_data.bio: Klinisch psycholoog and psychotherapeut at neurocare Clinics Den Haag, specializing in combined rTMS and psychotherapy treatment for depression and OCD.

name: neurocare Clinics Groningen
description_long: neurocare Clinics Groningen offers combined rTMS and psychotherapy treatment for depression and OCD in the northern Netherlands. As part of the neurocare group network with eight locations across the Netherlands, the Groningen clinic provides evidence-based brain stimulation therapies alongside comprehensive psychological support.

name: UMC Groningen - Universitair Centrum Psychiatrie
description_long: The Universitair Centrum Psychiatrie (UCP) at UMC Groningen provides rTMS through its Innovative Psychiatric Unit (IPU), offering specialized treatments for patients whose psychological complaints respond insufficiently to regular therapies. As an academic medical center, UMCG combines clinical rTMS treatment with innovative research including esketamine and VR therapy approaches.

name: neurocare Clinics Nijmegen
description_long: neurocare Clinics Nijmegen is one of eight neurocare locations in the Netherlands, offering combined rTMS and psychotherapy treatment. Located on Bijleveldsingel in the historic center of Nijmegen, the clinic provides comprehensive neuromodulation services in collaboration with the nearby Brainclinics Foundation research institute.
